title: practical english usage - Part 129
content:
I've got too many strawberries. Would you like some?

Nouns can be dropped after some, if the meaning is dear.

Some of us want to get to sleep, (     Some us...) not - Some people want to get to sleep, (     .. ,-some-of people...) not Dan's coming to some of the concerts, (     .. .some the concerts...) not - I've got tickets for some concerts next month, (     .. .-some ef concerts-. ..) pronoun, we  use some of Compare: Before another determiner (article, demonstrative or possessive word) or a 6 some      and   some      of;  some      with    no   following       noun The President has appealed for food for the earthquake victims.

- Bring some food in case we get hungry.

I like roses, (no idea of number) - We've planted some roses in the garden, (a limited number) Compare: limited quantity or number, we  do not usually use some. For details, ►     139.

indefinite (but not very large) quantity or number. When  there is no idea of a With an uncountable or plural noun, some usually suggests the idea of an 5 when     some      is  not   used I need some help, (uncountable noun) I need some new shirts, (plural countable noun) not / need a new coat, (singular countable noun) (     ... seme new eoat.) However, it is not normally used with the same kind of nouns. Compare: Some (in this sense) is used in similar ways to the indefinite article a/an (►   137).

4 some      and   a/an She hasn't got any manners.

- Did you meet any interesting people on the trip?

Do you mind if I put some music on?

- There are some children at the front door.

She's got no friends.

No (►   163)  means the same as not any, but is more emphatic.

She's unhappy because she hasn't got any friends, (     . . .-beeauseshedias-got not.

Any alone does not have a negative meaning. It is only negative when used with 3 any     and   not    any For details of the difference, ►     161 .

Sorry, I don't have any razor blades.

I need some razor blades. Do you have any razor blades?

Any often contrasts with some, which  is most common in affirmative clauses.

2 any     and   some For details of this use, ►     160.

You can borrow any book you want.

what/whieh'.

Any can be used to emphasise die idea of open choice: 'it doesn't matter who/ Perhaps you could correct any mistakes I've made.

Any fog will clear by noon. (= If there is any fog, it will clear by noon.) Sometimes any means 'if there is/are any* or 'whatever there is/are'.

If you find any blackberries, keep some for me.

Any is common after if.

1 suddenly realised I'd come out without any money.

The noise of the party prevented me from getting any sleep.

You never give me any help.

We didn't have any problems going through customs.

Is there any more coffee?

negative clauses, and in other cases where there is an idea of doubt or negation.

Because of its 'open*, non-specific meaning, any is often used in questions and is used when it is not important to say how  much/many we  are thinking of.

Any is a determiner. It generally suggests an indefinite amount or number, and 1  meaning:      indefinite       amount      or  number 159  any     _______________ For some time, sometime and sometimes, ►    590.

For somebody and anybody, something and anything, etc, ►     1 80.

We have exported some four thousand tons of bootlaces this year.

Some (/sʌm/) with a number suggests that the number is high or impressive.

In informal speech, some can show enthusiastic appreciation.

I don't want to spend my life doing some boring little office job.

Emily's gone to America to marry some sheep farmer or other.

Determiners:       Quantifiers «159 grammar          any For the use of any and no as adverbs, ►    373.    For any. . . but, ►    413.    For any and every, ►     162.

anything and anywhere. For more information about these, ►     180.

Many of the rules given above also apply to the compounds anybody, anyone, 7 compounds not ‘ How much  money do you have? ‘ None, (     Net any-.)' not ‘ What day should I come?' ‘ Any day. (     Any.)' We   don’ t use any or not any alone as answers.
